title: /explainCan you define and outline the ABC-X and D

parts.text: /explainCan you define and outline the ABC-X and Double ABC-x model for me
I'll explain the ABC-X and Double ABC-X models, which are foundational frameworks for understanding family crisis and adaptation.

input.back: A framework showing how crisis develops through interaction of three factors: (A) the provoking stressor event, (B) the family's resources, and (C) the meaning/perception attached to the stressor. These interact to produce (X) the crisis - a state of acute disequilibrium and immobilization.

An expanded crisis model that considers post-crisis adaptation. Double A = original stressor plus pileup of additional stressors. Double B = original resources plus newly acquired/strengthened resources. Double C = reframed perceptions of the entire situation. Double X = adaptation (long-term functional changes), not just stress reduction.

Positive long-term changes that occur in response to crises. Following crises, some individuals and families don't merely return to equilibrium - they become better able to function and handle future stress and crisis events. Also called stress-related growth or posttraumatic growth.
